key_insight: In crystalline HCP titanium under uniaxial compression, elastic response is size- and strain-rate-independent, but plastic deformation strongly depends on model size and strain rate through dislocation-mediated instability.
engineering_relevance: For atomistic compression simulations of titanium, choosing a sufficiently large cell is essential to obtain stable, realistic plasticity and to avoid misleading stress fluctuations at low strain rates.
method: System size varied across four orders of magnitude, up to 32 million atoms
Strain rates explored down to 10^8 s^-1
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic comparison across many system sizes and strain rates, showing unchanged elastic properties but pronounced size effects during plastic deformation.
watch_item: Results are from high-rate MD on crystalline models, so direct transfer to lower-rate or defect-rich experimental conditions may require caution.
paper_title: Uniaxial compression of crystalline HCP titanium: an atomistic modelling study of size effects
